properties.tree_description:
Tree is 5' from foundation with a compromised root system.  Most of the weight and limbs are directly over the roof of the home with an unbalanced crown. Reason for Removal:Uncorrectable defectComments:This tree has had several branch failures with roof damage.  A new roof was just put on the home. Additional pruning will not eliminate additional property damage.
		
	
	
                                
                            
                        
                    
                        
                            
                                
                                    
                                        
                                         Parcel Information
                                
                            
                            
                                
                                    
		Parcel Number:17 00640002052 *Block:--Lot:--Subdivision:--

The oak tree exhibits approximately 30% canopy dieback, indicating a decline in overall health and vitality. Visible symptoms suggest possible root rot, contributing to structural weakness and reduced stability.

Numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Tree appears dead 
		
		
			Tree number:3752Species:SycamoreTree Size (DBH):12Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:A basal cavity and an unbalanced canopy leaning toward the Falls Road bridge. Carpenter ant activity was observed within the cavity during probing, indicating advanced internal decay.
		
		
			Tree number:3753Species:PoplarTree Size (DBH):11Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Multi-stem coppice resprout originating from a previously cut stump. One stem confirmed dead, with remaining leaders forming a weak multi-stem structure.
		
		
			Tree number:3754Species:Mimosa Tree Size (DBH):10Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Mimosa leaning greater than 20 degrees toward the bridge structure, invasive undesired species.
		
		
			Tree number:3766Species:PoplarTree Size (DBH):6Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:The tree appears dead with no viable canopy present.
		
		
			Tree number:3767Species:Poplar Tree Size (DBH):12Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Structural root plate (SRP) erosion with associated root exposure is present, including evidence of prior root damage. The root zone is further impacted by the installation of geofabric and riprap, altering natural soil conditions and limiting root function. The tree also exhibits multiple leaders with rubbing contact, creating additional structural weakness.
		
		
			Tree number:3768Species:Sweetgum Tree Size (DBH):16Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Multi-stem structure consistent with basal coppice origin. Stems exhibit intertwining growth with included unions, reducing structural integrity. Evidence of prior root severance with associated decay is present, contributing to compromised stability.
		
		
			Tree number:3769Species:PoplarTree Size (DBH):27Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Structural root plate (SRP) erosion and undermining are present, with loss of supporting soil beneath the tree. Structural roots are exposed and in some cases no longer in contact with soil, indicating reduced anchorage. Early canopy decline is consistent with root system stress.
		
		
			Tree number:3744Species:PoplarTree Size (DBH):18Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Approximately 40% loss of structural root plate (SRP) due to active erosion. Erosion appears acute and localized, with undercutting of the bank and exposure of roots, including feeder roots. The condition reflects ongoing destabilization and reduced anchorage.
		
		
			Tree number:3785Species:Pine Tree Size (DBH):32Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Co-dominant stems are present and rubbing together, creating structural weakness. Subordinate leaders lean toward the retaining wall area of the adjacent apartment complex.
		
		
			Tree number:3792Species:Sweetgum Tree Size (DBH):18Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Mechanical damage and animal activity observed at the base, including beaver chewing. Two distinct mechanical wounds are present, one of which shows visible decay. Basal damage is contributing to compromised structural integrity and increased susceptibility to failure.
		
		
			Tree number:3804Species:Sweetgum Tree Size (DBH):8Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Large open basal wound with internal decay present. Damage appears consistent with wildlife chewing or mechanical disturbance, exposing decayed heartwood at the lower trunk.
		
		
			Tree number:3808Species:HWTree Size (DBH):14Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:numbered metal tag with white flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Sapwood chewed by wildlife approximately 80 percent of the circumference.
